title: Micro-environment of the Eu interstitial in $β$-SiAlON:Eu$^{2+}$ green phosphor
authors: Julien Bouquiaux, Samuel Poncé, Yongchao Jia, Masayoshi Mikami, Xavier Gonze
abstract: The precise atomic-scale structure around Eu$^{2+}$ activators in the $β$-Si$_{6-z}$Al$_z$O$_z$N$_{8-z}$:Eu$^{2+}$ commercial green phosphor remains elusive. We use the first-principles $Δ$SCF excited-state method, embedding of the interatomic force constants for supercells up to 3501 atoms, and Huang-Rhys theory to clarify this issue. Monte Carlo exploration is used to identify representative low-energy structural models spanning different levels of Al/O concentration $z$. For the lowest-energy structure at low $z$, our computed photoluminescence spectrum reproduces the experimental vibronic peaks at 6~K with excellent agreement in peak positions and intensities, validating the Eu-N$_9$ coordination model with Al, O, and Eu confined to the same crystallographic plane. Analysis of the low-energy structures reveals that the electron-phonon coupling is weak ($S \approx 2.15$) with a robust characteristic phonon signature across different Al/O arrangements, explaining the surprising persistence of resolved phonon replicas with increasing $z$. We explain the experimentally observed red-shift of emission with increasing $z$ through systematic trends in zero-phonon line energies, modest increases in Huang-Rhys factors, and larger configurational diversity at higher compositions.
summary: This paper uses first-principles calculations to determine the atomic structure of Eu2+ in beta-SiAlON green phosphors. It successfully reproduces experimental photoluminescence spectra and explains how changes in chemical composition lead to a red-shift in light emission. The work provides insight into how structural diversity and electron-phonon coupling drive optical properties in these materials.
main_problem: The precise atomic-scale structure and the origin of the red-shift in the green phosphor beta-SiAlON:Eu2+ remain unknown.
main_result: The study validates a Eu-N9 coordination model and explains the red-shift of emission through trends in zero-phonon line energies and increased configurational diversity as Al/O concentration increases.
method: First-principles delta-SCF excited-state method, embedding of interatomic force constants for large supercells, Monte Carlo structural exploration, and Huang-Rhys theory.
model_or_system: The system is the beta-Si6-zAlzOzN8-z:Eu2+ commercial green phosphor, specifically focusing on the micro-environment of the Eu2+ interstitial.
key_observables: Photoluminescence spectrum, vibronic peaks, zero-phonon line energies, and Huang-Rhys factors.
important_parameters: Al/O concentration (z), electron-phonon coupling strength (S approx 2.15), and temperature (6 K).
main_assumptions: The study assumes the validity of the delta-SCF method for excited states and uses representative low-energy structural models identified via Monte Carlo.
paper_structure: The paper identifies structural models via Monte Carlo, validates the Eu-N9 coordination model by comparing computed and experimental photoluminescence spectra, and analyzes electron-phonon coupling and emission shifts across different Al/O concentrations.
